name: Wild Goose
recipeIngredient: Celery stalk (1 long, cut into 2-inch pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the plucked and cleaned goose in well-salted water overnight.
2. Rinse and pat the goose dry, then stuff it with quartered onions and apples.
3. Place the breast side up in an open roaster pan and cover with 2 thick strips of bacon.
4. Brown the goose in a 475°F (245°C) oven until the bacon is crisp.
5. Remove all fat from the pan and discard it.
6. Remove the bacon.
7. Add quartered onion, carrot, celery, bay leaf, parsley, thyme, consommé with water, and red wine to the pan.
8. Cover and roast at 375°F (190°C) for 2 to 2½ hours, basting often.
9. The goose is done when the meat pulls away easily from the carcass.
10. Remove the goose and place it on a warm platter. Discard the stuffing.
11. Strain the gravy through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
12. Thicken the gravy with 1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed with 2 tablespoons cold water, stirring until smooth.
13. Adjust seasoning if necessary.
14. Serve with wild rice.
